questions.text: Which of the following is not possible according to Pauli's exclusion principle?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Electrons with same spin in same orbital
options.explanation: This option is not possible according to Pauli's exclusion principle, which prohibits two electrons from occupying the same orbital if they have the same spin.

options.text: Electrons with different spin in different orbital
options.explanation: This scenario is possible because different orbitals can accommodate electrons with different spins.

options.text: Electrons with same spin in different orbital
options.explanation: This situation is possible since electrons can have the same spin in different orbitals without violating the exclusion principle.

options.text: Electrons with different spin in same orbital
options.explanation: This option is possible because two electrons can occupy the same orbital as long as they have opposite spins.
This statement is incorrect because it implies that the first option is also possible, which it is not according to Pauli's exclusion principle.

questions.explanation: According to Pauli's exclusion principle, no two electrons in the same atom can have identical quantum numbers. This means that two electrons cannot occupy the same orbital with the same spin. Therefore, the correct answer is Electrons with same spin in same orbital, as this scenario is explicitly prohibited by the principle.In contrast, the other options describe scenarios that are permissible. Electrons can have different spins in the same orbital, and they can have the same spin in different orbitals without violating the principle. Thus, options B, C, D, and E are all valid scenarios.

questions.text: Which of the following spectral lines series lies in the visible Region?

options.explanation: This series lies in the ultraviolet (UV) region, with wavelengths between 100-400 nm.

The Balmer series is a series of spectral lines in the hydrogen atom spectrum that lies in the visible region of the electromagnetic spectrum, with wavelengths between 400-700 nanometers (nm). This series corresponds to electron transitions from higher energy levels (n ≥ 3) to the second energy level (n = 2).
This series lies in the infrared (IR) region, with wavelengths between 2.5-5 μm (micrometers).

questions.text: What is the radius of 3rd orbit of electron in H- atom.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: 3.39 Å is not a correct value for the radius of the 3rd orbit.

The radius of an electron orbit in a hydrogen atom can be calculated using the Bohr model, which gives the radius of the nth orbit as:rn = n² × 0.529 Åwhere n is the principal quantum number.For the 3rd orbit, n = 3, so:r₃ = 3² × 0.529 Å= 9 × 0.529 Å= 4.76 Å
5.29 × 10^11 Å is an extremely large value, far beyond the atomic scale.
9.7 Å is not a correct value for the radius of the 3rd orbit.

questions.text: _ are such nuclei of an element that have the same atomic number Z, but have different mass number.

questions.text: It is not possible for us to measure the exact position and exact momentum of electrons simultaneously is the statement of _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: De Broglie's statement, also known as the De Broglie hypothesis, states that every object or particle in motion, regardless of its mass, has a wave-like nature associated with it.
Moseley's statement, also known as Moseley's law, is a relationship between the wavelength of characteristic X-rays emitted by an element and its atomic number.

The Heisenberg uncertainty principle, also known as the uncertainty principle, is a fundamental principle in quantum mechanics that states that it is impossible to simultaneously determine certain pairs of physical properties of a quantum particle, such as its position and momentum, with arbitrary precision.

Niels Bohr was a Danish physicist who made important contributions to the development of atomic theory. One of his key contributions is the Bohr model of the atom, which describes the structure of atoms in terms of discrete energy levels.

questions.text: Positive rays are produced when _ strike the molecules of a gas in a discharge tube.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Low-speed neutrons: Neutrons are neutral particles, so they do not have any charge. They also have a very small mass, so they do not have enough energy to knock out electrons from gas molecules. Therefore, low-speed neutrons cannot produce positive rays.

High-speed neutrons: High-speed neutrons are also neutral particles, but they have a lot of energy. However, they are not affected by electric or magnetic fields, so they cannot be used to produce positive rays.
Low-speed electrons: Low-speed electrons do not have enough energy to knock out electrons from gas molecules. Therefore, low-speed electrons cannot produce positive rays.

Positive rays are produced when high-speed electrons strike the molecules of a gas in a discharge tube. When a high-speed electron strikes a molecule of gas, it can knock out one or more electrons from the molecule. The molecule that has lost electrons becomes a positively charged ion. These positively charged ions are called positive rays. The positive rays are attracted to the negative electrode in the discharge tube, and they can be deflected by a magnetic field. This deflection can be used to measure the charge-to-mass ratio of the positive rays.

questions.explanation: In the Discharge Tube Experiment, high-speed electrons transition from the metal of the cathode and go towards the anode. It may collide with gas molecules present in the tube. Out of many possibilities, one being the gas molecule may lose an electron (pushed out) upon collision, forming a stream of positively charged particles, also known as anode rays. Hence, the particles responsible for the production of positive rays are "high-speed electrons".

questions.text: Which of the following shows a black body's energy density?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The equation E=hcv shows the relationship between energy (E), Planck's constant (h), the speed of light (c), and the frequency (v) of electromagnetic radiation. Specifically, it states that the energy of a photon of electromagnetic radiation is equal to its frequency multiplied by Planck's constant. This equation is fundamental to the field of quantum mechanics and is used to describe the behaviour of light and other forms of electromagnetic radiation.

The equation E=c/v shows the relationship between the energy (E), speed of light (c), and wavelength (λ) of electromagnetic radiation. Specifically, it states that the energy of a photon of electromagnetic radiation is equal to its frequency multiplied by Planck's constant. This equation is fundamental to the field of quantum mechanics and is used to describe the behaviour of light and other forms of electromagnetic radiation.

The equation E=CV shows the relationship between the energy stored in a capacitor (E), the capacitance of the capacitor (C), and the voltage across the capacitor (V). Specifically, it states that the energy stored in a capacitor is equal to one-half of the product of its capacitance and the square of the voltage across it. This equation is important in the study of electrical circuits and is used to calculate the amount of energy stored in a capacitor at a given voltage.

The energy density of a black body between λ and λ + dλ is the energy E=hc/λ of mode times the density of states for photons, times the probability that the mode is occupied. This is Planck's famous formula for the energy density of a black body.

This equation is fundamental to the field of quantum mechanics and is used to describe the behaviour of light and other forms of electromagnetic radiation. Option B : The equation E=c/v shows the relationship between the energy (E), speed of light (c), and wavelength (λ) of electromagnetic radiation. Specifically, it states that the energy of a photon of electromagnetic radiation is equal to its frequency multiplied by Planck's constant. This equation is fundamental to the field of quantum mechanics and is used to describe the behaviour of light and other forms of electromagnetic radiation. Option C : The equation E=CV shows the relationship between the energy stored in a capacitor (E), the capacitance of the capacitor (C), and the voltage across the capacitor (V). Specifically, it states that the energy stored in a capacitor is equal to one-half of the product of its capacitance and the square of the voltage across it. This equation is important in the study of electrical circuits and is used to calculate the amount of energy stored in a capacitor at a given voltage. Option : D The energy density of a black body between λ and λ + dλ is the energy E=hc/λ of mode times the density of states for photons, times the probability that the mode is occupied. This is Planck's famous formula for the energy density of a black body.

questions.text: The region where the probability of finding the electron is negligible is called as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect option. Nucleus: The nucleus is the central part of an atom, containing protons and neutrons. The probability of finding an electron within the nucleus is very low, but not negligible in the same way as a nodal plane.

This is incorrect option. Atom: An atom is the basic building block of matter, consisting of a nucleus surrounded by electrons. Electrons are found within the atom, so the probability of finding them is not negligible.
This is incorrect option.Orbital: An orbital is a region within an atom where there is a high probability of finding an electron. It's the opposite of a nodal plane.

The region where the probability of finding an electron is negligible is called a nodal plane. A nodal plane is a region of space that passes through a nucleus where the probability of finding an electron is zero.

questions.text: According to 'n+I' rule ,the value of 6f is 9 while 7s orbital has '7' value, it shows?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: 6f has higher energy. Thus, it will be filled later.
Lower n+l value indicates lower energy orbitals which are filled first. Thus, 7s will be filled before 6f.
Lower n+l value indicates lower energy orbitals which are filled first. this option is incorrect.
Lower n+l value indicates lower energy orbitals which are placed first. this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The (n+l) rule corresponds to the Aufbau principle where n represents the quantum number and l represents the azimuthal number. The Aufbau principle states that electrons fill lower-energy atomic orbitals before filling higher-energy ones (Aufbau is German for "building-up"). A lower (n+l) value indicates lower-energy orbitals which are filled first. Hence, 7s will be filled before 6f.

questions.text: Which character of p-orbital determines the geometry of molecules?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This term typically refers to a flat, two-dimensional arrangement. In the context of molecular geometry, certain molecules exhibit planar geometry. For example, molecules with a central atom surrounded by three identical bonding pairs (trigonal planar) or molecules with four identical atoms or groups in a plane (square planar) have planar geometries. The p-orbitals may contribute to the overall geometry in such cases.

Axial generally refers to positions along a central axis. In molecular geometry, this term may be associated with the arrangement of atoms or groups in a specific direction. For instance, in molecules with a trigonal bipyramidal geometry, there are axial and equatorial positions. The axial positions are aligned along the axis perpendicular to the plane formed by the equatorial positions. P-orbitals can contribute to the axial positions in certain cases.

This term suggests that there is no specific direction associated with the character of the p-orbital. P-orbitals are generally directional in nature, but when they undergo hybridization, the resulting hybrid orbitals (such as sp3 hybrid orbitals) may have a more spherical distribution, reducing the directional nature of the original p-orbitals.

A p-orbital has two lobes like a dumbell and it has a strong directional character. There are three types of p-orbitals depending on the direction of lobes/their orientation in space along either x or y or z axis. When a bond is formed between two atoms by the overlap of two p orbitals or a p-orbital and an s orbital, the direction of the bond depends on the direction of p-orbital. A covalent bond is rigid and directional. The geometry of a molecule depends on the arrangement of the bonds and hence on the orientation of the orbitals involved in bonding.

questions.explanation: A p-orbital has two lobes like a dumbell and it has a strong directional character. There are three types of p-orbitals depending on the direction of lobes/their orientation in space along either x or y or z axis. When a bond is formed between two atoms by the overlap of two p orbitals or a p-orbital and an s orbital, the direction of the bond depends on the direction of p-orbital. A covalent bond is rigid and directional. The geometry of a molecule depends on the arrangement of the bonds and hence on the orientation of the orbitals involved in bonding.

questions.text: Quantum numbers specify the --------- of electrons.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The azimuthal quantum number (l) specifies the shape of the orbital (s, p, d, f) and is related to the angular momentum of the electron.
The principal quantum number (n) specifies the energy level or shell of the electron.
The magnetic quantum number (m) specifies the orientation and position of the orbital in space.

All of these options are correct.Quantum numbers specify the properties of the atomic orbitals and the electrons in those orbitals. An electron in an atom or ion has four quantum numbers to describe its state. Think of them as important variables in an equation that describe the three-dimensional position of electrons in a given atom.

questions.explanation: Quantum numbers specify different properties of electrons in an atom. Specifically, the four quantum numbers (principal, azimuthal, magnetic, and spin) specify the energy, shape, orientation, and spin of an electron, respectively.The principal quantum number (n) specifies the energy level or shell of the electron.The azimuthal quantum number (l) specifies the shape of the orbital (s, p, d, f) and is related to the angular momentum of the electron.The magnetic quantum number (m) specifies the orientation of the orbital in space.The spin quantum number (s) specifies the spin of the electron.Therefore, quantum numbers specify the energy, shape, position, and spin of electrons in an atom. ALL THESE OPTIONS ARE CORRECT

questions.text: The region where probability of finding the electron is maximum is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The probability of finding electrons in the nucleus is not maximum, but rather very close to zero. This is because the electrons are repelled by the positively charged nucleus, and the probability of finding an electron within the nucleus is very low. The probability of finding electrons is highest in the regions of the orbital closest to the nucleus.

The probability of finding electrons in an atom is not maximum but rather depends on the specific electron configuration of the atom. The electrons are distributed in different orbitals around the nucleus, and the probability of finding an electron in a particular region of space depends on the electron density of that region. The electron density is the probability distribution of the electrons, and it is highest in the regions of the orbital closest to the nucleus.

The region where the probability of finding electrons is maximum is called the orbital. Orbitals are regions of space around an atom or molecule where there is a high probability of finding an electron. The shape and size of an orbital depend on the energy level and quantum numbers of the electron. Different types of orbitals have different shapes and orientations, such as s, p, d, and f orbitals.

The probability of finding electrons in the nodal plane is zero, as the nodal plane is a region of space where the probability of finding electrons is exactly zero. The nodal plane is a flat surface that divides the orbital into two parts with opposite signs of electron density. Therefore, the probability of finding electrons is highest in the regions of the orbital closest to the nucleus, and it is zero in the nodal plane.

questions.explanation: The region where the probability of finding electrons is maximum is called the orbital. Orbitals are regions of space around an atom or molecule where there is a high probability of finding an electron. The shape and size of an orbital depend on the energy level and quantum numbers of the electron. Different types of orbitals have different shapes and orientations, such as s, p, d, and f orbitals.

questions.text: Quantum numbers are set of numerical values which give solutions to the equation of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Quantum numbers are not used to solve the Planck equation. The Planck equation, also known as Planck's law, is a fundamental equation in physics that describes the spectral density of electromagnetic radiation emitted by a black body at a certain temperature. It is used to describe the behavior of light and other forms of electromagnetic radiation.

Quantum numbers are not used to solve the Heisenberg equation. The Heisenberg equation, also known as the Heisenberg uncertainty principle, is a fundamental principle of quantum mechanics that states that it is impossible to simultaneously measure certain pairs of physical properties of a particle with perfect accuracy.

Quantum numbers are a set of numerical values that describe the properties of electrons in an atom. These numbers are used to solve the Schrödinger equation, which is a mathematical equation that describes the behavior of electrons in an atom. The Schrödinger equation is a fundamental equation of quantum mechanics and is used to calculate the energy levels and wave functions of electrons in an atom. The quantum numbers provide a way to label and describe the different energy levels and orbitals that electrons can occupy in an atom.

questions.text: Canal rays are produced in the discharge tube from -------
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The anode is the positively charged electrode in the discharge tube. Canal rays are not produced from the anode.
The cathode is the negatively charged electrode in the discharge tube.

Canal rays are produced in a discharge tube when gas molecules in the tube are ionized and accelerated towards the anode by a high voltage applied between the anode and cathode. So the correct option is "Gas molecules in tube".
The correct answer is c) Cathode.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since the electrons emitted from the 'cathode' collide with the gas atoms found in the tube, knocking one or two additional electrons out of each of these atoms. These collisions leave behind ions that are positively charged. The produced positive ions travel towards the cathode. Hence canal rays are produced from gas molecules in the tube.

questions.text: According to Bohr's theory electron should move _ nearer to nucleus in an orbit of _ radii.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Slower, smaller: This option is incorrect because the speed of the electron and the radius of its orbit are inversely proportional. This means that as the speed of the electron increases, the radius of its orbit decreases.

The answer is faster and smaller. According to Bohr's theory, the speed of an electron in an atom is inversely proportional to the radius of its orbit. This means that as the radius of the orbit decreases, the speed of the electron increases.The reason for this is that the electron is constrained by the electromagnetic force of the nucleus. The closer the electron is to the nucleus, the stronger the force, and the faster the electron must move to stay in orbit.The radius of an electron's orbit is also related to its energy level. The lower the energy level, the smaller the radius, and the faster the electron must move. This is because the lower energy levels are closer to the nucleus, and therefore the electromagnetic force is stronger.Therefore, if an electron moves nearer to the nucleus, it will be moving faster and have a smaller radius.

Faster, bigger: This option is incorrect because the speed of the electron and the radius of its orbit are inversely proportional. This means that as the speed of the electron increases, the radius of its orbit decreases.

Slower, bigger: This option is incorrect because the speed of the electron and the radius of its orbit are inversely proportional. This means that as the speed of the electron increases, the radius of its orbit decreases.

questions.explanation: Correct option is B.The answer is faster and smaller. According to Bohr's theory, the speed of an electron in an atom is inversely proportional to the radius of its orbit. This means that as the radius of the orbit decreases, the speed of the electron increases. The reason for this is that the electron is constrained by the electromagnetic force of the nucleus. The closer the electron is to the nucleus, the stronger the force, and the faster the electron must move to stay in orbit.The radius of an electron's orbit is also related to its energy level. The lower the energy level, the smaller the radius, and the faster the electron must move. This is because the lower energy levels are closer to the nucleus, and therefore the electromagnetic force is stronger.Therefore, if an electron moves nearer to the nucleus, it will be moving faster and have a smaller radius.

questions.text: Which of the following is the electronic configuration of sodium11?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The electronic configuration of sodium (Na) is not [He]2s1. The [He] part represents the noble gas configuration of helium, and sodium has additional electrons beyond helium.
[Ne]3s1 is the electronic configuration of sodium. The [Ne] part represents the noble gas configuration of neon, and the 3s1 indicates that there is one electron in the 3s orbital.
The electronic configuration [Ne]3s2 would represent magnesium (Mg), which has two electrons in the 3s orbital. Sodium (Na) has only one electron in the 3s orbital.
The electronic configuration [He]2s2 would represent helium (He) or the helium ion. Sodium (Na) has more electrons than helium.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct since sodium11 has 11 electrons whereas Ne has 10 electrons.The [Ne] part represents the noble gas configuration of neon, and the 3s1 indicates that there is one electron in the 3s orbital.

questions.text: When an electron changes its orbit from outer to inner level, energy is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When an electron changes an orbit, energy does change.
When an electron jumps from a lower energy level to a higher energy level, energy is absorbed.
When an electron emits energy it becomes stable and jumps from a higher energy level (outer shell) to a lower energy level (inner shell) hence the energy is released.
During the transition between the energy levels, energy does not remain constant.

questions.explanation: When an electron emits energy it becomes stable and jumps from a higher energy level (outer shell) to a lower energy level (inner shell) hence the energy is released.

questions.text: The line spectrum of sodium contains _ colored lines separated by a definite distance.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) One yellow:This option suggests that the line spectrum of sodium contains one yellow-colored line separated by a definite distance. This statement is not correct. The line spectrum of sodium actually consists of multiple lines, not just one, and they are not all yellow.

Remember that the line spectrum of sodium contains two yellow coloured lines separated by a definite distance. The number of lines and the distance between the lines in any line spectrum depends upon the element volatilized.

c) Two brown:This option suggests that the line spectrum of sodium contains two brown-colored lines separated by a definite distance. This statement is not correct. The line spectrum of sodium does not have brown lines.

d) Two golden:This option states that the line spectrum of sodium contains two golden-colored lines separated by a definite distance. This statement is also not accurate. The line spectrum of sodium does not have golden lines.

questions.explanation: Remember that the line spectrum of sodium contains two yellow colored lines separated by a definite distance. The number of lines and the distance between the lines in any line spectrum depends upon the element volatilized.

questions.text: Sp hybrid orbitals are formed by the mixing of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This combination results in sp2 hybrid orbitals, not sp. An sp2 hybridization involves one s and two p orbitals forming three equivalent sp2 orbitals, each with 33.3% s character and 66.7% p character.
This combination results in sp3 hybrid orbitals. An sp3 hybridization involves one s and three p orbitals forming four equivalent sp3 orbitals, each with 25% s character and 75% p character.

Correct! In sp hybridization, one s and one p orbital combine to form two sp orbitals, each having 50% s character and 50% p character. This configuration is ideal when an atom is bonded to two other groups.
This configuration does not exist in common hybridization schemes. Hybridization typically involves the mixing of one s orbital with one or more p orbitals.

questions.explanation: In sp hybridization, one s orbital and one p orbital hybridize to form two sp orbitals, each possessing 50% s character and 50% p character. This type of hybridization is typical in linear molecular geometries, where the central atom is bonded to two other atoms. Option A describes sp2 hybridization, Option B describes sp3 hybridization, and Option D is not a valid hybridization scheme.

questions.text: The nature of positive rays depends on:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The composition or material of the electrode does not influence the formation or nature of positive rays.

options.text: The nature of the discharge tube
options.explanation: The shape, size, or material of the discharge tube does not affect the nature of the positive rays produced.

options.text: The nature of the residual gas.
options.explanation: Positive rays are composed of ions of the residual gas in the discharge tube. The type of gas determines the characteristics of the positive rays, including their mass and charge.
The geometric shape of the electrode is irrelevant to the nature of positive rays.

questions.explanation: Positive rays, also known as canal rays, are produced in a discharge tube and consist of positively charged ions originating from the residual gas present in the tube. The characteristics of these rays, such as their mass-to-charge ratio, depend on the type of gas used. This is why the nature of the residual gas is crucial. The other factors, such as the nature or shape of the electrode and the design of the discharge tube, do not affect the composition or properties of the positive rays, as they do not influence the ionization of the gas.

questions.text: Tritium, an isotope of hydrogen contains:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Equal number of electrons and neutrons
options.explanation: As per explanation, this is incorrect.

options.text: Equal number of electrons, protons and neutrons
options.explanation: As per explanation, this is incorrect.

options.text: Number of neutrons is double than the number of protons
options.explanation: Tritium is the isotope of the hydrogen having mass number 3 and proton number 1.Number of neutrons are mass number-number protons which turn out to be 2. So we can say that the number of neutrons is twice the number of protons.

options.text: Number of neutrons is half than the number of protons
options.explanation: As per explanation, this is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Tritium is the isotope of the hydrogen having mass number 3 and proton number 1.Number of neutrons are mass number-number protons which turn out to be 2. So we can say that the number of neutrons is twice the number of protons.

questions.text: The atomic number Z of an atom represents the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The atomic number Z of an atom represents the number of protons in the nucleus. Atomic number Z is equal to the number of electrons only for neutral atoms whereas for ions the number of electrons will be different from the number of protons. So, Z is the number of protons and our correct option is A.

The atomic number Z of an atom represents the number of protons in the nucleus. Atomic number Z is equal to the number of electrons only for neutral atoms whereas for ions the number of electrons will be different from the number of protons. So, Z is the number of protons and our correct option is A.
Z represents the number of protons. Number of neutrons N can be calculated by the formula N= A-Z where A is the mass number of the atom.

The sum of protons and neutrons in an atom is known as the mass number (A) of the atom. The mass number represents the total number of nucleons (protons and neutrons) present in the nucleus of the atom.Mass number (A) = Number of protons (Z) + Number of neutrons (N)

questions.explanation: The atomic number Z of an atom represents the number of protons in the nucleus. Atomic number Z is equal to the number of electrons only for neutral atoms whereas for ions the number of electrons will be different from the number of protons. So, Z is the number of protons and our correct option is A.

questions.text: The neutron was discovered by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: German physicist, Goldstein is known for his work on electrical phenomena in gases and on cathode rays, thus this is incorrect option.
Early in the century, Ernest Rutherford developed a crude model of the atom, based on the gold foil experiment and discovered the existence of nucleus, hence this option is incorrect
J.J Thomson is credited with the discovery of the electrons,hence this option is incorrect.
Chadwick discovered neutrons in 1932 and Goldstein discovered canal ray in 1886. Thus, this is the correct option.

questions.explanation: Thomson discovered electrons in 1897, Chadwick discovered neutrons in 1932 and Goldstein discovered canal ray in 1886.

questions.text: Electronic configuration of M2+ ion is 2, 8, 14 and its atomic mass is 56 amu. The number of neutrons in its nucleus are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Total number of electrons in M2+ ions are 2+8+14=24, since they lost two electrons to become this ion that means neutral M will have 26 electrons. Since neutral atoms have the same numbers of electrons and protons so the number of protons in M is also 26. As the question states that the mass number of M2+ is 56. Mass number, being the sum of protons and neutrons, can be used to calculate neutrons, by subtracting protons from it(56-26=30).
Number of neutrons is 30 not 32.
Number of neutrons is 30 not 42.
Number of neutrons is 30 not 52.

questions.explanation: Total number of electrons in M2+ ions are 2+8+14=24, since they lost two electrons to become this ion that means neutral M will have 26 electrons. Since neutral atoms have the same numbers of electrons and protons so the number of protons in M is also 26. As the question states that the mass number of M2+ is 56. Mass number, being the sum of protons and neutrons, can be used to calculate neutrons, by subtracting protons from it(56-26=30)

questions.text: The amount of energy released by adding an electron in the valence shell is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom or ion. The first ionization energy of an atom or molecule is the energy required to remove one mole of electrons from one mole of isolated gaseous atoms or ions.

The electron affinity is the energy released when an electron is added to a neutral gaseous atom to form a negative ion. The more negative the electron affinity the more favorable the electron addition process is. Option B is correct as it corresponds to this definition.
Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract shared electrons to itself.
Atomic radius is generally stated as being the total distance from an atom’s nucleus to the outermost orbital of the electron.

Atomization energy, or enthalpy of atomization, ΔaH0, is the change in enthalpy when one mole of bonds is completely broken to obtain atoms in the gas phase. For example atomization of methane molecules. CH4 (g) → C(g) + 4H(g) ΔH= 1665.0 kJ mol-1

questions.explanation: Electron affinity is defined as the amount of energy released when an electron is added to the valence shell of a gaseous, neutral, and isolated atom.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs has the same electronic configuration as possessed by Neon (Ne-10)?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium (Na+) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^1, while chlorine (Cl-) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3p^5. The configuration of chlorine is different from the electronic configuration of neon (Ne-10), which is 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

Potassium (K+) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3p^6 4s^1, while chlorine (Cl-) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3p^5. These configurations are different from the electronic configuration of neon (Ne-10). Therefore, this pair does not have the same electronic configuration as neon.
Potassium (K+) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3p^6 4s^1. While, sodium has same electronic configuration as neon, but potassium does not. So, this option is imcorrect.

Sodium (Na+) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^1, while fluorine (F-) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^5. Na+ loses one electron and F- ions gain one electron from their valence shells, resulting in the same electronic configuration as neon. Therefore, this pair has the same electronic configuration as neon.

questions.explanation: Sodium (Na+) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^1, while fluorine (F-) has the electronic configuration 1s^2 2s^2 2p^5. Na+ loses one electron and F- ions gain one electron from their valence shells, resulting in the same electronic configuration as neon. Therefore, this pair has the same electronic configuration as neon.

questions.text: There are four orbitals s, p, d and f. Which order is correct with respect to the increasing energy of the orbitals?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to the order of increasing energy, the s orbitals have lower energy than the p orbitals. However, the d orbitals have higher energy than the p orbitals, and the f orbitals have higher energy than the d orbitals. Therefore, the correct order should be: 4s < 4p < 4d < 4f.
This order is incorrect because it suggests that the f orbitals have lower energy than the d orbitals, which is not the case.
This order is incorrect because it suggests that the f orbitals have lower energy than the p orbitals, which is not the case
This order is incorrect because it suggests that the f orbitals have lower energy than the s orbitals, which is not the case

questions.explanation: According to the order of increasing energy, the s orbitals have lower energy than the p orbitals. However, the d orbitals have higher energy than the p orbitals, and the f orbitals have higher energy than the d orbitals. Therefore, the correct order should be: 4s < 4p < 4d < 4f.

questions.text: The weight of an electron is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electrons are negatively charged particles weighing zero atomic mass units and located in the various orbitals of the energy levels outside the atomic nucleus. The electron actually weighs 9.11x10-30 grams. This means it would take about 1,830 electrons to equal the mass of one proton.
9.108 × 10−25 g: This option also represents a weight that is too heavy for an electron. Again, the actual weight of an electron is much smaller.
9.108 × 10^−31 g: This option represents the approximate weight of an electron. It is in the range of the actual mass of an electron, which is approximately 9.109 × 10^−31 kg.

Electrons are negatively charged particles weighing zero atomic mass units and located in the various orbitals of the energy levels outside the atomic nucleus. The electron actually weighs 9.11x10-28 grams. This means it would take about 1,830 electrons to equal the mass of one proton.

questions.explanation: 9.108 × 10-30 g: This option represents the approximate weight of an electron. It is in the range of the actual mass of an electron, which is approximately 9.109 × 10-30 kg.

questions.text: The nature of cathode rays in discharge tube:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Depends on the nature of gas taken in the discharge tube
options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because cathode rays are independent of the nature of the gas in the discharge tube. They exhibit similar properties regardless of the type of gas present.

options.text: Depends upon the nature of cathode in discharge tube
options.explanation: Option B is incorrect because cathode rays are not dependent on the nature of the cathode itself. They originate from the cathode, but their fundamental properties are determined by the nature of electrons and their behavior in the electric field.

options.text: Is independent of the nature of the gas in discharge tube
options.explanation: Cathode rays in the discharge tube are the electrons produced due to ionisation of gas and that emitted by cathode due to collision of positive ions and are hence independent of nature of the gas in discharge tube.

options.text: Depends upon the nature of anode in the discharge tube
options.explanation: Option D is incorrect because cathode rays are not influenced by the nature of the anode either. Anodes typically function to attract the electrons emitted by the cathode, but they do not alter the fundamental characteristics of cathode rays.

questions.explanation: Cathode rays in the discharge tube are the electrons produced due to ionisation of gas and that emitted by cathode due to collision of positive ions and are hence independent of nature of the gas in discharge tube.

questions.text: Cathode rays travel in discharge tube from:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cathode rays are those rays produced from the negatively charged cathode present in the discharge tube and are actually beams of electrons that travel in straight lines from cathode to anode.
Cathode rays are those rays produced from the negatively charged cathode present in the discharge tube and are actually beams of electrons that travel in straight lines from cathode to anode.
Cathode rays are those rays produced from the negatively charged cathode present in the discharge tube and are actually beams of electrons that travel in straight lines from cathode to anode.
Cathode rays are those rays produced from the negatively charged cathode present in the discharge tube and are actually beams of electrons that travel in straight lines from cathode to anode.

questions.explanation: Cathode rays come from the cathode because the cathode is charged negatively. So, those rays strike and ionize the gas sample inside the container. Therefore, cathode rays travel in discharge tube from cathode to anode.

questions.text: Which pair has 1 electron in its ‘s’ orbital?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: -Lithium (Li) has 3 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s¹]. One electron occupies the 's' orbital. - Iron (Fe) has 26 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 4s² 3d⁶]. Iron has electrons filling both the 's' and 'd' orbitals, but it doesn't have just one electron in the 's' orbital.

- Sodium (Na) has 11 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s¹]. One electron occupies the 's' orbital. - Chromium (Cr) has 24 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 4s¹ 3d⁵]. In the case of chromium, the electron configuration shows that one electron occupies the 's' orbital.

- Potassium (K) has 19 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 4s¹]. One electron occupies the 's' orbital. - Manganese (Mn) has 25 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 4s² 3d⁵]. However, it doesn't have only one electron in the 's' orbital.

- Hydrogen (H) has 1 electron in its electron configuration [1s¹]. This electron occupies the 's' orbital. - Helium (He) has 2 electrons in its electron configuration [1s²]. Both electrons fill the 's' orbital.

questions.explanation: - Sodium (Na) has 11 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s¹]. One electron occupies the 's' orbital. - Chromium (Cr) has 24 electrons in its electron configuration [1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 4s¹ 3d⁵]. In the case of chromium, the electron configuration shows that one electron occupies the 's' orbital.

options.explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom or ion. The first ionization energy of an atom or molecule is the energy required to remove one mole of electrons from one mole of isolated gaseous atoms or ions.

The electron affinity is the energy released when an electron is added to a neutral gaseous atom to form a negative ion. The more negative the electron affinity the more favorable the electron addition process is. Option B is correct as it corresponds to this definition.
Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract shared electrons to itself.
Atomic radius is generally stated as being the total distance from an atom’s nucleus to the outermost orbital of the electron.

Atomization energy, or enthalpy of atomization, ΔaH0, is the change in enthalpy when one mole of bonds is completely broken to obtain atoms in the gas phase. For example atomization of methane molecules. CH4 (g) → C(g) + 4H(g) ΔH= 1665.0 kJ mol-1

questions.explanation: Electron affinity is defined as the amount of energy released when an electron is added to the valence shell of a gaseous, neutral, and isolated atom.

questions.explanation: Correct option is D. Atomicity is defined as the number of atoms in the molecules of an element. Atomicity refers to the number of atoms present in a single molecule of an element, substance or compound. It can also be considered as the nature of molecules.
